summary: Added SWIFT_PLATFORM_TYPES allowlist and EXCLUDED_PATH_PREFIXES to ios-accuracy-validator.py. Both false positives cleared. iOS drift: 0.

changes.change: Added EXCLUDED_PATH_PREFIXES = ('uikit/', 'social-plus-sdk/video-new/') — mirrors Android/Flutter validators. Files under these paths are skipped entirely. Clears Bundle.main.path false positive from uikit/customization/component-styling.mdx.

Added SWIFT_PLATFORM_TYPES set (34 entries: stdlib, Foundation, UIKit, SwiftUI, concurrency). When a dotted ref's type_name is in this set, the ref is skipped. Clears String.self false positive from edit-comment.mdx. Applied in scan_swift_block after KNOWN_VALID_REFS check.

allowlist_added: String, Int, Double, Float, Bool, Array, Dictionary, Set, Optional
Bundle, URL, Data, Date, FileManager, NotificationCenter, UserDefaults, DispatchQueue, OperationQueue, Timer, JSONDecoder, JSONEncoder
UIView, UIViewController, UIImage, UIColor, UIApplication, UINavigationController, UITableView, UICollectionView, UILabel, UIButton
View, Text, Image, Button, NavigationView, List, VStack, HStack, ZStack

false_positive_analysis.String.self: SWIFT_PLATFORM_TYPES — String is stdlib, .self is a metatype expression, not an SDK member
false_positive_analysis.Bundle.main.path: EXCLUDED_PATH_PREFIXES — file is uikit/customization/component-styling.mdx, UIKit SDK scope

notes.allowlist_too_broad_risk: Low. The allowlist only skips refs whose type_name matches exactly. A ref like 'String.someAmityExtension' would still be flagged because String appears in the surface type pattern only if a type named 'String' were in ios.json — which it is not, so the dotted-ref regex wouldn't fire at all for stdlib types. Wait: the type_name_pat regex is built only from surface type names, so platform types not in surface would never be matched in the first place. The SWIFT_PLATFORM_TYPES guard is a belt-and-suspenders defense for cases where a future SDK surface accidentally adds a 'String' shadow type. Current surface has no stdlib name collisions.
